description: Fail-fast bring-up flow for promoting PTO and SuperNPUBench AI workloads from source contracts through Linx LLVM, QEMU, and the C++ LinxCoreModel.

artifact_policy.rule: AI workload bring-up artifacts must be placed under workloads/generated/<run-id>/ai-bringup/ or a temp directory, never as ad-hoc sibling directories under workloads/.

qemu.rule: Prefer the current in-repo build-linx QEMU binary for local AI bring-up unless QEMU or a matching clean-build marker explicitly selects another binary.

smoke.description: Tier-0 proof that source discovery, bounded PTO parity/tile smoke, and minimal SuperNPUBench tileop cases can enter the flow.

pr.description: Fast promotion lane for Tier 0 and Tier 1 AI workload bring-up.

nightly.description: Expanded AI workload lane for matrix, memory, reduction, accelerator, and model suites.

full.description: Full AI workload matrix. Required final target is all promoted cases passing in C++ LinxCoreModel.

avs_pto.description: AVS direct-boot PTO/tile suites that produce a Linx ELF through avs/qemu/run_tests.py.

pto_kernel.description: Cataloged PTO kernel sources. Most entries validate source and compile/static contracts only; explicitly promoted final-green direct-boot smoke ELF cases include Tier-1 pto-kernel-tload_store, pto-kernel-gemm, pto-kernel-gemm_basic, pto-kernel-gemm_demo, pto-kernel-gemm_performance, pto-kernel-gemm_reuse_a_fp16, pto-kernel-gemm_reuse_b_fp16, pto-kernel-gemm_reuse_ab_fp16, pto-kernel-mamulb, pto-kernel-tmatmul_acc, pto-kernel-relu_fp32, and pto-kernel-add_custom, plus Tier-2 pto-kernel-flatten_fp32, pto-kernel-reshape_fp32, pto-kernel-squeeze_fp32, pto-kernel-unsqueeze_fp32, pto-kernel-concat_fp32, pto-kernel-split_fp32, pto-kernel-stack_fp32, pto-kernel-permute_nhwc_nchw_fp32, pto-kernel-transpose_large_fp32, pto-kernel-slice_fp32, pto-kernel-gather_fp32, pto-kernel-scatter_fp32, pto-kernel-where_fp32, pto-kernel-argmax_fp32, pto-kernel-unique_i32, pto-kernel-hash_table_insert_fp32, pto-kernel-hash_table_lookup_fp32, and pto-kernel-unsorted_segment_sum_fp32. Float add_custom and unsorted_segment_sum use harness-local freestanding __addsf3 helpers scoped to positive integer-valued smoke inputs seeded by the oracle; they are not general compiler-rt replacements. The gemm_basic/gemm_demo/gemm_performance smokes are float bit-pattern copy-oracles for PTO_QEMU_SMOKE branches, not full float TMATMUL/TCVT/TMULS coverage; gemm_performance keeps repeat_tiles=3 and verifies the final repeat with a precomputed expected-bit table. The FP16 reuse smokes use harness-local positive-integer __mulsf3/__addsf3 shims and require --model-timeout 600 for current final-green AI flow evidence.

supernpu.description: SuperNPUBench Makefile cases compiled with PLAT=linx, per-case OBJ_ROOT under the run artifact tree, and the in-repo Linx LLVM toolchain.

tiers.name: smoke
tiers.description: Tiny known-good Linx AVS smoke, bounded PTO parity tload_store smoke, tile smoke, and minimal SuperNPUBench tileop cases.

tiers.name: kernel-pr
tiers.description: Standalone PTO catalog smoke harnesses, PTO kernel catalog compile/static checks, full smoke-sized AVS PTO parity maturity coverage, and basic SuperNPUBench tile/kernel families.

tiers.name: ai-kernel-domains
tiers.description: Matrix, reduction, memory, and accelerator cases with digest/trace capture when available.

tiers.name: model-workloads
tiers.description: DeepSeek/model-oriented cases and larger shapes suitable for nightly runs.

tiers.name: full-matrix
tiers.description: Full PTO and SuperNPUBench matrix for final LinxCoreModel maturity closure.

stages.why: Stop before compiler or model work if PTO catalogs, SuperNPUBench manifests, source paths, or normalized case records are invalid.
stages.promotes: valid cases with source hashes and reproducible build metadata

stages.why: Stop before QEMU if the in-repo Linx LLVM toolchain cannot compile, link, or statically inspect the selected cases.
stages.promotes: Linx ELF for executable cases, standalone PTO harness cases, and assembly/object/static reports for non-executable PTO kernel cases

stages.why: Stop before model execution if QEMU cannot run the legal compiler-produced Linx artifact.
stages.promotes: QEMU-passing Linx ELFs with log, optional trace, and digest evidence

stages.why: Stop before model workload execution if the C++ LinxCoreModel cannot build or run a known generated smoke ELF within the model runtime timeout.
stages.promotes: fresh gfsim binary plus generated model-smoke source, linker script, ELF, and bounded smoke transcript

stages.why: Final execution target: run only QEMU-passing ELFs through model/LinxCoreModel/bin/gfsim.
stages.promotes: model-passing cases or model-owned fix packets with stop reason, log, optional trace, digest evidence, finisher/assertion markers, and latest BROB head progress

stages.why: Compare QEMU and model evidence, then assign first-failure owners using the stage ownership map.
stages.promotes: owner-classified failures and green final cases

stages.why: Emit bounded fix packets for benchmark, compiler, emulator, model, and docs-skills agents.
stages.promotes: per-failure reproduction packets with artifact links and expected next boundary

stages.why: Close every run with a skill-evolve update/no-update decision and document reusable flow changes.
stages.promotes: skill-evolve closeout line and documentation delta summary

case_selection.contains_match: --case <text> selects cases whose id, suite, or kind contains <text>.
case_selection.exact_match: --case =<text> selects only cases whose id, suite, or kind equals <text>; quote the argument in zsh shells.

supernpu_source_selection.generic_testcase_rule: When a SuperNPUBench compile.all row uses a generic TESTCASE such as matmul, resolve concrete source files from TYPE before TESTCASE and preserve actual path casing in source manifests.
supernpu_source_selection.example: kernel/gemm/matmul TESTCASE=matmul TYPE=HIF4_HIF4 resolves src/HiF4_HiF4.cpp, not src/matmul.cpp or a case-mismatched path.

supernpu_compiler_contract.evidence: SuperNPUBench Linx tile API implementation is not available
SuperNPUBench source uses unsupported Linx tile runtime contract
markers: Linx smoke TCOPYIN supports only unboxed tiles
Linx scalar MATMUL supports only unboxed layouts
Linx scalar MATMUL does not support ACC tile operands

supernpu_compiler_contract.evidence: SuperNPUBench source is not adapted to the Linx direct-boot runtime

model_diagnostics.rule: When gfsim fails or times out, report.json and fix packets should include the parsed terminal marker or latest BROB head progress if the log provides it.
